prs.title: Added the Links for raw and external data used for the project
fix: handle dynamic multiple attribute on select
prs.body:
Clo​ses #17280 _(edit: it does not)_

This PR fixes an issue where the [selected](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/tests/runtime-runes/samples/select-spread-option-selected:0:0-0:0) property of options in a `<select>` element would not update correctly when the [multiple](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/tests/validator/samples/select-multiple:0:0-0:0) attribute was toggled dynamically (e.g., `<select {multiple}>`).

### The Problem
Previously, the runtime `MutationObserver` in [select.js](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/src/internal/client/dom/elements/bindings/select.js:0:0-0:0) only watched for changes to the [value](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/tests/runtime-runes/samples/select-falsy-value:0:0-0:0) attribute. It ignored changes to the [multiple](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/tests/validator/samples/select-multiple:0:0-0:0) attribute. Consequently, when [multiple](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/tests/validator/samples/select-multiple:0:0-0:0) changed from `false` to `true` (or vice versa), the selection logic was not re-evaluated, leading to inconsistent DOM states where options had [selected](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/tests/runtime-runes/samples/select-spread-option-selected:0:0-0:0) attributes but [selected](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/tests/runtime-runes/samples/select-spread-option-selected:0:0-0:0) properties set to `false`.

Additionally, the compiler explicitly forbade using a dynamic [multiple](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/tests/validator/samples/select-multiple:0:0-0:0) attribute with `bind:value`, throwing an [attribute_invalid_multiple](cci:1://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/src/compiler/errors.js:767:0-774:1) error.

### The Solution
1.  **Runtime**: Updated the `MutationObserver` in [packages/svelte/src/internal/client/dom/elements/bindings/select.js](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/src/internal/client/dom/elements/bindings/select.js:0:0-0:0) to include `'multiple'` in its `attributeFilter`. This ensures the selection logic runs whenever the [multiple](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/tests/validator/samples/select-multiple:0:0-0:0) attribute changes.
2.  **Compiler**: Removed the [attribute_invalid_multiple](cci:1://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/src/compiler/errors.js:767:0-774:1) validation in [BindDirective.js](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/src/compiler/phases/2-analyze/visitors/BindDirective.js:0:0-0:0) and its definition in [errors.js](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/src/compiler/errors.js:0:0-0:0), allowing dynamic [multiple](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/svelte/packages/svelte/tests/validator/samples/select-multiple:0:0-0:0) attributes to be used with `bind:value`.

## Tests

- Added a new regression test case: `packages/svelte/tests/runtime-runes/samples/select-multiple-dynamic-attribute`.
    - This test toggles the `multiple` attribute and asserts that `select.multiple` and `option.selected` properties update correctly.
- Removed the obsolete validator test `binding-select-multiple-dynamic` which asserted the now-removed error.
- Verified that all select-related tests in `packages/svelte/tests/runtime-runes` pass.

## Checklist

- [x] It's really useful if your PR references an issue where it is discussed ahead of time.
- [x] Prefix your PR title with `feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`, or `docs:`.
- [x] This message body should clearly illustrate what problems it solves.
- [x] Ideally, include a test that fails without this PR but passes with it.
- [x] If this PR changes code within `packages/svelte/src`, add a changeset (`npx changeset`).
- [x] Run the tests with `pnpm test` and lint the project with `pnpm lint`.

prs.title: Fix #20420: Prevent double decoding of file URL parameter
prs.body:
Fixes #20420

### Description
This PR fixes a regression where passing a URL with encoded parameters (e.g., `?token=%2Ffoo`) to the viewer would fail due to incorrect double decoding.

*   **The Bug:** In [web/app.js](cci:7://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/pdf.js/web/app.js:0:0-0:0), the `file` parameter retrieved from `URLSearchParams` was being passed to `decodeURIComponent`. Since `URLSearchParams.get()` already decodes values, this caused a second round of decoding. This corrupted URLs containing encoded characters (like `%2F` becoming `/`), which broke signed URLs or paths requiring encoding.
*   **The Fix:** Removed the redundant `decodeURIComponent` call. The `file` parameter is now passed directly to the [URL](cci:1://file:///Users/uzairahmedshah/Downloads/Uzair/Development_Coding%20Files/Web%20Development/HTML,CSS,%20JS/Projects/pdf.js/web/app.js:2422:2-2445:4) constructor, preserving the correct encoding.

### Testing
Verified manually using a reproduction case:
1.  **Input:** `https://example.com/pdf?token=%2Ffoo`
2.  **Before Fix:** Resulted in `...token=/foo` (Incorrect, broke the token).
3.  **After Fix:** Results in `...token=%2Ffoo` (Correct, preserves encoding).

prs.title: Fix layout shift in search bar by grouping results (Issue #34650)
prs.body:
Summary
Fixed a layout shift issue in the "Find Channels" (Ctrl+K) modal. Previously, the "Channels" header would flicker or disappear when switching between local and remote search results, causing the entire list to jump.

This change ensures that 
SwitchChannelProvider
 consistently uses the "Channels" label and returns grouped results for both local and remote searches, maintaining a stable UI layout.

QA Test Steps:

Open the "Find Channels" modal (Cmd+K or Ctrl+K).
Type a search term that fetches remote results (e.g., a public channel name not in your history).
Verify that the "Channels" header appears immediately and remains stable without causing the list to shift up or down as results load.

Ticket Link
Fixes https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/issues/34650

**Release Note**

```release-note
Fixed a layout shift in the search bar where the 'Channels' header would flicker or disappear when loading remote results.

prs.title: Update Driver.jsx and BusCard.jsx: Logout button placement and Quick …
prs.body:
Moved the logout button from the Driver dashboard to the BusCard component for better accessibility. Passed onLogout as a prop to BusCard to handle logout from within the component. Updated state handling in Driver.jsx to clear user details and reset views on logout.
Fixes #86

prs.title: Add footer with correct routing using react-router-dom
prs.body:
Replaced anchor () tags in the footer with React Router components for proper client-side routing. Footer layout remains responsive and fixed at the bottom.
Fixes #63

prs.title: Added HLD.md – High-Level Design for Backend #fixes #4
prs.body:
## 🚀 Pull Request Summary

This PR adds the `HLD.md` file, which outlines the **High-Level Design for the Driver Status & Location Tracking** feature in the Shuttle Tracker system.

### 📌 Key Highlights:

- Implements a lightweight in-memory approach to temporarily store the driver’s current location (`lat`, `lng`)
Fixes #4
